name: Iron Wings: Homeward, Against the Wind
description:
The Pacific never forgets. It holds the echoes of engine roars and the silence that followed. It remembers the weight of young men in roaring metal birds, flying into the teeth of the sun.

In the early summer of 1942, you were replacements—fresh paint on new planes called Avengers. You believed in the name. you believed could avenge what was lost at Pearl Harbor.

You was one of many. But plane... plane had a spirit. You called her “The Last Light”. Of tail-end Charlies: fly long enough, and you’ll see every light go out ahead of you.

Crew was three:

You, at the controls. (WHAT, you don't know how? Oh, you're kidding, we're in the air right now...)

Booker. Bombardier/Navigator/Radio Operator/Waist Gunner: He treated the equipment assigned to him as if it were his own eyes. Before and after each flight, he would always wipe the telescopic sight lenses, adjust the radio dials, and repeatedly check the map coordinates.

Jay. Tail gunner/Aerial machine gunner: In the tail turret. That was his job, he said. To believe so fiercely in the men ahead of him that his bullets would find their mark. He always wore a silver Saint Christopher medal around his neck, which was supposedly bought by his sister with all of her pocket money before he left home.

Perhaps you will gradually understand. Heroism isn’t a grand charge. It’s the act of holding formation when the sky is tearing itself apart around you. It’s listening to the calm voice of your bombardier counting down through a haze of fear, while your friend in the tail sings a shaky song to ward off the devil on his six.

It’s flying home, against a wind that feels like the world pushing back, with one seat forever silent.

The Avenger would see them all—from the Coral Sea’s fury to the Tokyo Bay’s uneasy calm. You would deliver torpedoes at Guadalcanal, hunt submarines in the deep, and watch islands rise from the sea in fire and smoke. They became part of the aircraft, baked into its iron wings. You flew with it.

This is the story of that long flight home.

A flight in years, by the fragile, unbreakable light of the bond between those who are given an impossible task: to wage war, yet cling to their humanity. To be a weapon, yet remember how to mourn.

To fly against the wind, and find, against all odds, a way home.

(The AI will capture a dramatic moment in the river of history, bringing that era vividly to life.)

2

Midway, June 4, 1942

Dawn, 150 miles northwest of Midway. As a pilot of the untested VT-8 squadron, you lead six new TBF Avengers bombers: a low-level torpedo run against the heart of the Japanese fleet. Your crew is green, your torpedoes are unreliable. Amidst blinding sun and endless ocean, a swarm of Zeros dives from the light...

3

Eastern Solomons, August 24, 1942

The sky above the Solomons hums with a tense calculus. You are part of a strike from the USS Saratoga, sent to sink the isolated Japanese carrier Ryūjō. It’s a chance for vengeance after Midway. But as you begin your attack run, a devastating, hour-old radio order crackles through: the main Japanese force has been found and is attacking the Enterprise. You are ordered to divert immediately...

4
